mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance against a zero-mean null hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered months or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises below the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series in out-of-sample tests or produces negative returns when both series grow concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production reflect weakening manufacturing output that compresses corporate profit margins, generating negative equity returns specifically during late-cycle economic expansions.
failure_condition: The negative signal reverses to positive returns during late-cycle periods or shows no directional consistency when industrial production contracts.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 4.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, producing negative forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in over 60% of observations when the rate exceeds the threshold or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls alongside a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns or underperforms a buy-and-hold benchmark over consecutive quarterly windows.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that directly translates to higher corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon regardless of short-term market noise.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to predict monthly equity direction with a Rank IC above 0.04 or produces negative returns during expansionary quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during post-shock recovery phases indicate lingering supply chain bottlenecks that disrupt earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive returns during post-shock contractions or loses statistical significance when industrial production declines exceed the threshold.
